name: Militia
notes: Off 2/Def 2 per Seventeenth Session Canon (2026-04-25). Basic universal troop — not primitive, world has basic ironworking from start.

name: Swordsman
notes: Off 4/Def 2 per Seventeenth Session Canon (2026-04-25). Basic offensive infantry. Requires basic iron capability.
Carries the early reconnaissance role in active canon.
Separate Scout unit was removed from early roster canon.

name: Archer
notes: Canonical name updated 2026-04-25: Archers (Off 3/Def 2). Prior name 'Bowman' is deprecated as primary name, retained as ID for backward compatibility.
Longer range than Boltmen. Less armor-piercing than Boltmen. Standard ranged pressure, anti-light infantry.

name: Spearmen
notes: Canonical Seventeenth Session unit (2026-04-25). Off 2/Def 4. Basic defensive infantry and early anti-cavalry unit.
Defense-first but can attack when needed. Critical for preventing cavalry dominance.
antiCavalryBonus applies against mounted units in formed defensive stance.

name: Shieldbearer Swordsmen
notes: Canonical Seventeenth Session unit (2026-04-25). Off 5/Def 3. Swordsmen upgraded with shields.
Offense-leaning, more survivable than basic Swordsmen. First major improvement in the offensive infantry line.
Upgrade path from Swordsmen. Requires shield equipment and basic armory access.

name: Heavy Swordsmen
notes: Canonical Seventeenth Session unit (2026-04-25). Off 6/Def 4. Armored sword infantry.
Better armor, larger swords, more disciplined fighting. Serious core unit. Not yet elite.
Upgrade path from Shieldbearer Swordsmen. Requires armory (upgraded), iron supply.

name: Greatsword Knight
notes: Canonical Seventeenth Session unit (2026-04-25). Off 8/Def 3. Elite offensive foot unit without horseback.
Line-breaking, high damage, offense-dominant. More vulnerable than Bulwark Guard. Less balanced than Knight.
Requires advanced armory and trained melee specialists. Not the best defensive elite.

name: Spear Guard
notes: Canonical Seventeenth Session unit (2026-04-25). Off 3/Def 5. Improved version of Spearmen.
Shielded or better-equipped. Stronger line-holding. Better defensive reliability. Prior name 'Shielded Spearmen' is superseded.
Upgrade path from Spearmen. Requires training grounds and shield equipment.

name: Pikeguard
notes: Canonical Seventeenth Session unit (2026-04-25). Off 4/Def 6. Advanced defensive polearm unit.
Improved long weapons, better formation discipline. Strong against cavalry. Prior names 'Armored Pikemen' and 'Pike Square' are superseded.
Upgrade path from Spear Guard. Requires armory and formation training.

name: Bulwark Guard
notes: Canonical Seventeenth Session unit (2026-04-25). Off 3/Def 8. Elite defensive foot unit.
Holds ground, protects lines, resists formation-breaking pressure. Not meant to kill quickly — value is staying power.
Protects archers, siege units, bloodline members, and vulnerable troops. Prior name 'Shield Wall' is superseded.
Requires advanced armory and defensive training specialization.

name: Boltmen
notes: Canonical Seventeenth Session unit (2026-04-25). Off 5/Def 2. Crossbow unit. Canonical name: Boltmen.
Shorter range than Archers (90 vs 122). Stronger armor-piercing (armorPiercingBonus 1.5). Faster reload than historical heavy crossbow.
Anti-armored infantry, anti-shielded units. 'Crossbowmen' is acceptable as descriptive alias only.
Upgrade path from Archers. Requires armory and crossbow training.

name: Mounted Swordsmen
notes: Canonical Seventeenth Session unit (2026-04-25). Off 5/Def 2. Early cavalry.
Men with swords on horseback. Fast, offense-oriented, not heavily protected. Not a Knight.
Speed, harassment, pursuit, flanking, open-field pressure.
Requires horse/stable branch or equivalent early cavalry infrastructure.
Do not confuse with Scout Rider (existing stage 2 recon/raid cavalry unit).

name: Mounted Heavy Swordsmen
notes: Canonical Seventeenth Session unit (2026-04-25). Off 6/Def 3. Upgraded mounted swordsmen.
Shields and better equipment make the difference. More survivable than Mounted Swordsmen. Still not a Knight.
Prior name 'Shielded Mounted Swordsmen' is superseded.
Requires improved cavalry infrastructure, shields, and better mounted equipment.

name: Horse Archer
notes: Canonical Seventeenth Session unit (2026-04-25). Off 3/Def 2. Mounted ranged harassment unit.
Limited range (78 vs Archers 122) — shooting from horseback is harder. High mobility, weak if caught.
Mobile harassment, raiding, skirmishing, pursuit. Should not replace foot Archers.
Prior names 'Mounted Bowman' and 'Mounted Bowmen' are deprecated.
Requires horse/stable branch plus ranged training.

name: Mounted Knight
notes: Canonical Seventeenth Session unit (2026-04-25). Off 7/Def 5. Heavy mounted cavalry. Canonical name: Mounted Knight.
Horse, armor, shield, sword, and lance. Not spammable. Major power from grouped lance charge / break-formation, not just base stats.
lanceChargeEnabled: grouped formation charge attack pending full formation system implementation.
lanceChargeRequiresGrouped: charge most powerful when Mounted Knights are grouped and properly formed.
Countered by braced Spearmen, Spear Guard, Pikeguard. Poor in forests, tight ruins, steep terrain, cramped urban spaces.
canDismount: may dismount into Knight unit. Remounting requires controlled/friendly conditions (pending implementation).
Requires advanced cavalry infrastructure, warhorses, advanced armor/iron, shields, swords, lance training.

name: Knight
notes: Knight is produced by dismounting a Mounted Knight, not recruited independently. cost.gold=0 reflects the no-direct-recruitment path.
Canonical Seventeenth Session unit (2026-04-25). Off 6/Def 6. Dismounted form of Mounted Knight. Canonical name: Knight.
Versatile armored elite infantry. Slower, no mounted charge. 'Dismounted Knight' is acceptable as explanatory text only.
Does not outperform Greatsword Knight offensively. Does not outperform Bulwark Guard defensively.
Balanced, durable, useful in terrain where mounted combat is poor (sieges, rough terrain, cramped spaces).
canRemount: remounting requires controlled/friendly conditions, timer, and preserved horse. Not instant. Pending full implementation.

name: Axeman
notes: Off 5/Def 2 per Seventeenth Session Canon (2026-04-25). Updated from Ninth Ingestion Canon (Off 6/Def 4). Only Off/Def values revised — all other Ironmark identity (Ferocity Under Loss, blood cost, axe symbol, anti-armor bonus, weakness to fast units) remains intact.
Session 69 enables Axeman as the first live Ironmark unique unit.
Axeman carries a heavier blood levy and blood-production load than standard Ironmark infantry, tying house identity back into Ironmark's civilizational cost lane.
Ironmark specialized offensive variation on the swordsman/offensive infantry line. More offense-focused, less defensively reliable than shielded/armored sword troops.

name: Verdant Warden
notes: Canonical Hartvale unique unit with Off 4 / Def 5 profile, now live behind Hartvale house-gated prototype access.
Heavier health pool and armor than Ironmark Axeman reflecting defensive-tendency house identity.
Food cost component reflects Hartvale's pastoral identity. Session 11 added to data, Session 63 enables live prototype training.

name: Scout Rider
notes: Canonical first cavalry unit, light reconnaissance and raid arm of stage 2 warfare.
Live Session 79 layer: can disable undefended enemy infrastructure, strip local resources, and depress march loyalty.
Live Session 80 layer: can harry worked seams directly, scatter nearby workers, and force local counter-raid response.
Live Session 81 layer: can intercept moving logistics carriers, starting with supply wagons, and break active siege and field-water sustainment.

name: Tribal Raider
notes: Neutral tribe pressure unit for frontier camp control
Not trainable by dynasty factions in the first playable slice

name: Battering Ram
notes: Canonical first siege engine. Primary purpose: gate and wall-base breach.
Structural multiplier 3.5 counters wall 0.2 multiplier for net 0.7x effective wall damage.
Anti-unit damage reduced to 0.4 because ram is a structural weapon requiring escort.

name: Siege Tower
notes: Slow wall-height assault engine that supports nearby infantry pushing a defended wall line.
Nearby allies attacking structures gain better assault reach and more effective structural pressure.

name: Trebuchet
notes: Counterweight bombard engine for wall and tower degradation before a breach assault.
Long-range structural pressure that remains inefficient against protected combat units.

name: Siege Engineer
notes: Dedicated siege specialist rather than standard infantry.
Provides breach assessment, engine tending, earthwork support, and repair for nearby siege assets.

name: Supply Wagon
notes: Forward siege-line logistics unit carrying food, water, and replacement parts.
Nearby siege engines lose operational efficiency when wagons or their linked camp are cut off.
Live Session 81 layer: scout-rider interception can temporarily interdict the wagon, strip carried stores, and break active convoy sustainment.

name: Ballista
notes: Ranged anti-personnel siege-support engine. Between bowman and trebuchet in range.
Anti-unit multiplier is boosted over other engines because ballista targets formed infantry approaching the siege line.
Structural multiplier is moderate: ballista can pressure towers and gatehouses but is not a primary wall-breaker.

name: Flame Warden
notes: Session 20 canonical Old Light Light-path L3 unit. Vigilant frontline sentinel.

name: Judgment Pyre
notes: Session 20 canonical Old Light Dark-path L3 unit. Aggressive judgment-bringer.

name: Consecrated Blade
notes: Session 20 canonical Blood Dominion Light-path L3 unit. Oathsworn elite melee.

name: Crimson Reaver
notes: Session 20 canonical Blood Dominion Dark-path L3 unit. Sacrificial-rite raider.

name: Mandate Sentinel
notes: Session 20 canonical Order Light-path L3 unit. Stewardship-line defender.

name: Edict Enforcer
notes: Session 20 canonical Order Dark-path L3 unit. Submission-edict executioner.

name: Root Warden
notes: Session 20 canonical Wild Light-path L3 unit. Rootbound ranged warden.

name: Predator Stalker
notes: Session 20 canonical Wild Dark-path L3 unit. Predator-claim ambusher.

name: Flame Herald
notes: Session 21 canonical Old Light Light-path L4. Sanctified herald of the First Flame.

name: Pyre Sovereign
notes: Session 21 canonical Old Light Dark-path L4. Pyre-crowned inquisitor of judgment.

name: Blood Consort
notes: Session 21 canonical Blood Dominion Light-path L4. Oath-bound consort of the Red Covenant.

name: Crimson Exarch
notes: Session 21 canonical Blood Dominion Dark-path L4. Exarch of sacrificial war.

name: Mandate Paladin
notes: Session 21 canonical Order Light-path L4. Paladin-of-Stewardship apex defender.

name: Edict Inquisitor
notes: Session 21 canonical Order Dark-path L4. Inquisitor enforcing the edict of submission.

name: Elder Grove Keeper
notes: Session 21 canonical Wild Light-path L4. Grove keeper channeling Thorned Elder Roots.

name: Predator Ascendant
notes: Session 21 canonical Wild Dark-path L4. Ascended predator-claim hunter-king.

name: The Unbroken
notes: Session 22 canonical Old Light L5 apex unit. One-per-match per canon. The Unbroken flame-bearer.

name: The Sacrificed
notes: Session 22 canonical Blood Dominion L5 apex unit. One-per-match per canon. The Sacrificed oath-incarnate.

name: The Mandate
notes: Session 22 canonical Order L5 apex unit. One-per-match per canon. The Mandate-incarnate inviolable.

name: The First Wild
notes: Session 22 canonical Wild L5 apex unit. One-per-match per canon. The First Wild Thorned-Elder incarnation.

name: Fishing Boat
notes: Session 27 canonical vessel class 1 of 6 per 11_MATCHFLOW/NAVAL_SYSTEM.md.
Movement restricted to water tiles via movementDomain.
Gathers food from water tiles canonically (fishing). First naval economic vessel.

name: Scout Vessel
notes: Session 27 canonical vessel class 2 of 6. Fast, long-sight scout for naval reconnaissance.

name: Transport Ship
notes: Session 28 canonical vessel class 4 of 6.
Carries up to 6 land units across water. Transport mechanic itself remains DOCUMENTED pending the embark/disembark layer.

name: Fire Ship
notes: Session 28 canonical vessel class 5 of 6. One-use sacrifice vessel.
Canonical: Fire Ship detonates on first strike (oneUseSacrifice) and is destroyed along with its target.
Sacrifice detonation mechanic remains DOCUMENTED pending the one-use combat layer.

name: Capital Ship
notes: Session 28 canonical vessel class 6 of 6. Apex-tier naval capital.
Long-range bombardment, heavy armor, anchor of any naval doctrine posture. Canonical apex sea power per master doctrine section XV.

name: War Galley
notes: Session 27 canonical vessel class 3 of 6. First naval combatant — ranged, medium speed, meaningful armor.

name: Mantlet
notes: Mobile cover that reduces inbound ranged damage to nearby friendly siege crew, engineers, and other combat units.
Does not attack. Provides a directional cover zone for siege lines advancing against fortified positions.
Canonical successor to trench-and-screen protection: absorbs arrow, bolt, and light bombardment volleys.
